ships.name: KGS Hauler IV
ships.description: Although the Hauler, Hauler II, and Hauler III are more popular, the Hauler IV is still used by many, for its low price, durable hull, and excellent cargo space. It is, however, very slow.

ships.name: KGS Hauler V
ships.description: The Hauler V has even more cargo space than the Hauler IV, but it is even slower. It is, however, very durable.

ships.name: KGS Hauler VI
ships.description: The Hauler VI has an incredible amount of cargo space, but it is equally slow. It is only used because it is cheaper than two Hauler IIIs.

ships.name: KGS Hauler VII
ships.description: The Hauler VII is the largest freighter built by Southbound Shipyards. It lives up to its title with over 900 tons of cargo space, while being cheaper than multiple smaller freighters.

ships.name: KGS Maker
ships.description: A Rainmaker without its missile launchers, the Maker is a sturdy but sluggish interceptor. Because of its decreased firepower, the Maker is available to regular civilians for purchase.

ships.name: KGS Dipper
ships.description: The Dipper is a light warship and freighter cobbled together from two Clipper hulls. Seven gun ports and a spacious cargo hold make it a desirable ship among merchants and pirates in the South.

ships.name: KGS Mistmaker
ships.description: The Mistmaker is lighter version of the Rainmaker missile boat, built for civilian use. It comes equipped with four missile pods and enough ammunition to destroy even ships many times its size, and is also slightly faster than the Rainmaker.

ships.name: KGS Nightingale
ships.description: The Nightingale is a modified Raven with 24 gun ports. Such a huge modification resulted in lowered shield efficiency and hull structural integrity, along with higher drag and worse heat dissipation.

ships.name: KGS Sparrowhawk
ships.description: Tarazed's Sparrowhawk-class light warship was made by mashing together their interceptors, the Hawk and the Sparrow. The result is a powerful ship, with six guns and decent mobility. It has become very popular as an affordable escort for captains fending off pirates in the South.

ships.name: KGS BIG PEW PEW
ships.description: The Lionheart Raven is their most popular warship, an agile and elegant vessel that can nevertheless deal out and absorb considerable damage. Ravens are a favorite ship for bounty hunters, because they are more than a match for pirate interceptors and can be used by a skilled pilot to take down much larger ships as well.

ships.name: KGS Eyas
ships.description: A shortened Kingfisher, the Eyas-class Destroyer is the fastest medium warship in the Southern Rim, although it is somewhat undergunned.

ships.name: KGS Eyrie
ships.description: The Lionheart Eyrie is a light carrier derived from the Aerie. The Aerie's four internal fighter bays have been rearranged into six drone bays, which are commonly found carrying Lionheart's Stiletto drones.

ships.name: KGS Stormmaker
ships.description: The Stormmaker is a heavy missile platform derived from the Rainmaker missile boat. It comes equipped with twelve missile launchers and enough ammunition to destroy ships many times its size. However, it is still slow, and its backup non-missile armament is still very weak.

ships.name: KGS Kingfisher
ships.description: A Falcon without its side pods, the Kingfisher-class Battlecruiser is faster and even sleeker, at the cost of weapon space and hardpoints. It has been adapted to use strike tactics, flying fast at the target and unloading a flurry of plasma fire and Javelins before retreating for another dive.

ships.name: KGS Kookaburra
ships.description: Tarazed Shipyards created this monstrosity as a competitor to the Syndicate Protector. A Kingfisher has been stretched, and 10 extra turrets have been added. What it lacks in affordability it makes up for in mobility - upgraded engines allow it to go alarmingly fast for a 12 turret ship.

ships.name: KGS Fallcoon
ships.description: The Tarazed Fallcoon was designed as a competitor to Southbound Shipyards' Dreadnought. Its twin Falcon hulls boast huge engine bays and a staggering array of Plasma Cannons. Although its design was completed too late for it to take part in the civil war, it is still a terrifying warship, able to go head-to-head with a Cruiser.

ships.name: KGS Raindrop
ships.description: The Raindrop is a light missile drone built in the Republic Navy Yard, equipped with two missile launchers. Because of its extremely frail hull and lack of shielding, Raindrops serve best as long-range attack drones, and once their missile supply is spent, they are useless in combat.

ships.name: KGS Stiletto
ships.description: The Stiletto is a drone developed by Lionheart, derived from the Dagger fighter. As with almost all Lionheart ships, its hull is very light. A potent shield matrix along with long range missile pods give it above average survivability for a drone. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

ships.name: KGS Yestrel
ships.description: Two years after the design of the Kestrel, Tarazed's chief ship designer has finally completed the design of a ship even more powerful, the Yestrel.

ships.name: KGS Biolofez
ships.description: The Biolofez was made by integrating the hulls of two salvaged 'olofez. The result is a quite capable interceptor. Due to its low fuel capacity and lack of shield generation, the Remnant use it only for patrol and point defense.

ships.name: KGS Triolofez
ships.description: A Biolofez with another 'olofez tacked on, the Triolofez is a very powerful interceptor. However, its low fuel capacity combined with the fact that the Remnant do not make dedicated shield generation or hull repair have relegated it to in-system patrol and point defense.

ships.name: KGS Yolofez
ships.description: The Yolofez is two 'olofez hulls put together, but with the cockpit of the upper 'olofez modified into a turret mount. The result is a good interceptor. However, due to its low fuel capacity and lack of recovery systems, the Remnant use it only for patrol and point defense.

ships.name: KGS Silver Penguin
ships.description: Inspired by close contact with void sprites and the reinvigoration of xenobiology, the Penguin is a prototype designed to serve the dual roles of testing new starship technology while exploring hostile planets throughout the Ember Waste. One of this ship's distinguishing features is that it is truly alive and has a mind that approaches self-awareness. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Penguin's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

ships.name: KGS Silver Puffin
ships.description: Centuries ago, the Remnant built these ships in order to explore the upper atmosphere of nearby gas giants. The Archons took issue with the Remnant's research methods, and the studies were unable to be completed. The remaining Puffins have been gathering dust in storage for centuries while the Remnant wait to see if the anger of the Archons will subside.

ships.name: KGS Silver Gull
ships.description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

ships.name: KGS Silver Pelican
ships.description: The Pelican is closely based on the original semi-organic materials the Remnant found in the Ember Waste. While they are not technically alive, their internal mechanisms and method of construction bear such a close resemblance to living organisms that they are often considered as such. One key feature of this living resemblance is the innate sensory capabilities they possess, akin to sensor suites less advanced ships require for detailed scanning. These ships serve as the backbone of Remnant bulk salvage efforts, as well as their fast-logistics distribution network.

ships.name: KGS Silver Merganser
ships.description: The Merganser is the first production ship the Remnant deem to be truly alive, and are considered to be equivalent to dolphins in reasoning tests. These interceptors, deemed for short range operations by Remnant standards, are designed to patrol the upper atmosphere of gas giants and use their decisive speed advantage to conduct hit-and-run attacks on threats in nearby systems. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Merganser's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

ships.name: KGS Silver Starling
ships.description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

ships.name: KGS Silver Ibis
ships.description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

ships.name: KGS Silver Peregrine
ships.description: The Peregrine is the first capital warship designed by Taely using fresh insights garnered from recent experimentation as well as renewed studies of the void sprites. Encompassing over a century of development since the last generation of warships was built, it is the forerunner of a new breed of Remnant ships. 	The Peregrine was designed along the lines of old-earth's fast-attack cruisers. Drawing on inspiration from its namesake, famed for being the fastest self-propelled lifeform on Earth, it is designed to strike hard and fast. It closes quickly with enemies to unleash devastating barrages in the opening seconds of combat. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Peregrine's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

ships.name: KGS Silver Albatross
ships.description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

ships.name: KGS Silver Petrel
ships.description: The Petrel is a multi-role mining/wing leader fightercraft designed to provide support for flights of lighter Terns in high-risk environments. The ship's design originated as an urban mobile gun platform that flitted around structures and natural terrain, but proved easy to adapt into a spacecraft. 	The Remnant are loath to sacrifice pilots and crew from the comparatively small pool they are drawn from. As such, their fighters are equipped with medium-range weaponry designed to strike enemies from afar. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

ships.name: KGS Silver Smew
ships.description: The Remnant have long favored turns of action that avoid putting crew directly at risk; though their Terns are a rather effective result of this ideology, the Remnant still had to face the fact that, eventually, one of their manned ships was going to end up too crippled to escape to safety. To that end, the Smew was developed; though quite lightly armed, Smews are capable of carrying a considerable quantity of cargo and passengers for a ship of their size, and are durable enough to absorb some fire from enemies while doing so. 	Though originally designed as lifeboats, the surprisingly mobile freighter-fighter hybrid has seen a remarkable amount of usage in mining fleets, as a way to supplement the otherwise poor cargo capacity of the Ibis. Because their purpose can vary from fleet to fleet, Smews are traditionally sold with a fair amount of free space: enough for a small weapon, upgraded engines, or additional support systems. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

ships.name: KGS Silver Tern
ships.description: The Tern is a small, hardy drone developed to plunge into the atmosphere of nearby gas giants for collecting valuable resources. These thick-shelled craft are also surprisingly effective when pressed into service as screening ships when enemies intrude into Remnant space. 	Unlike most spacefaring nations, the Remnant do not have enough people to pilot fighter wings in waves likely to sustain significant casualties. As such, they frequently use these hardy drones to fulfill similar screening roles in their home systems, allowing manned craft to focus on more specialized tasks. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

ships.name: KGS Rapid Stream
ships.description: Developed after the Unfettered invasion, the Rapid Stream is a cut down Deep River, equipped with three turrets for extra defense from Unfettered Trackers.

variants.name: Chigiriki (KGS Twin Mandible Cannon)
variants.description: The Chigiriki is the largest of the Bunrodea's gunships, boasting an astonishing seventeen gun ports. One of these ports is equipped with the devastating Thorax Cannon, a massive cannon capable of dealing significant damage to any ship that dares get in the Chigiriki's way. Some Chigiriki also double as missile platforms, raining down walls of missiles so thick that they can blot out the nearest star.

variants.name: Grasshopper (KGS Hyperpulse Cannon)
variants.description: Designed by the Hai to serve as a fast reconnaissance craft, the Grasshopper has been adopted by thrill-seekers and miners alike. At the same time, it is often used by Hai captains who are looking for effective protection at a low cost.

variants.name: Sea Scorpion (KGS Bipulse Ripper)
variants.description: The Sea Scorpion was designed several decades ago to serve as a mobile platform to launch Trackers. However, it is only recently that the Unfettered have been able to mass-produce them. While individually vulnerable, the reduced cost of manufacturing compared to Shield Beetles allow the materially inferior Unfettered to gain a numerical advantage against their brethren.

variants.name: Shield Beetle (KGS Hyperpulse Cannon)
variants.description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

variants.name: Shield Beetle (KGS Bipulse Ripper)
variants.description: This ship was first built millennia ago, near the end of a more violent time in Hai history. Since then, it has served as the only heavy warship at the disposal of the Hai. The Shield Beetle is a versatile warship used by the Hai to guard their merchant convoys, or by the Unfettered to attack those very same convoys.

variants.name: Shuttle (KGS Mini Blaster)
variants.description: Although Betelgeuse Shipyards produces other ship models as well, the majority of their profits come from sales of the Shuttle. This versatile ship serves equally well as a passenger transport or a cargo courier. It also happens to be the cheapest ship you can buy which is capable of hyperspace travel. 	Shuttles are not designed to withstand combat of any sort, but they are fast and maneuverable enough to get out of harm's way if attacked by a larger, slower ship. Although they are typically unarmed, they have enough space for one weapon, which is the origin of the popular phrase, "as useless as a blaster cannon on a shuttlecraft."

variants.name: Shuttle (KGS Javelin Micro Pod)
variants.description: Although Betelgeuse Shipyards produces other ship models as well, the majority of their profits come from sales of the Shuttle. This versatile ship serves equally well as a passenger transport or a cargo courier. It also happens to be the cheapest ship you can buy which is capable of hyperspace travel. 	Shuttles are not designed to withstand combat of any sort, but they are fast and maneuverable enough to get out of harm's way if attacked by a larger, slower ship. Although they are typically unarmed, they have enough space for one weapon, which is the origin of the popular phrase, "as useless as a blaster cannon on a shuttlecraft."

variants.name: Scout (KGS Particle Carronade)
variants.description: The Lionheart Scoutship was designed back in the days when the galaxy was more sparsely populated, and ships prospecting for habitable worlds or worlds rich in resources would often travel far away from any habitable ports where they would be able to refuel. Although the galaxy has now been much more thoroughly explored, the Scout remains a popular ship for long-distance travel.

variants.name: Argosy (KGS Strike)
variants.description: The Argosy from Southbound Shipyards is very popular with merchants not because of its capacity, which is actually slightly smaller than the Syndicate Freighter, but because it can hold its own in combat even against a small pirate fleet. 	The Argosy is also a very easy ship to upgrade with faster engines or more powerful weapons as needed, so many privately owned Argosies have very different capabilities than the stock model.

variants.name: Argosy (KGS Twin Gatling)
variants.description: The Argosy from Southbound Shipyards is very popular with merchants not because of its capacity, which is actually slightly smaller than the Syndicate Freighter, but because it can hold its own in combat even against a small pirate fleet. 	The Argosy is also a very easy ship to upgrade with faster engines or more powerful weapons as needed, so many privately owned Argosies have very different capabilities than the stock model.

variants.name: Argosy (KGS Staggered Twin Gatling)
variants.description: The Argosy from Southbound Shipyards is very popular with merchants not because of its capacity, which is actually slightly smaller than the Syndicate Freighter, but because it can hold its own in combat even against a small pirate fleet. 	The Argosy is also a very easy ship to upgrade with faster engines or more powerful weapons as needed, so many privately owned Argosies have very different capabilities than the stock model.

variants.name: Clipper (KGS Storm)
variants.description: The Clipper is the smaller sister ship to the popular Southbound Shipyards Argosy. Although not able to carry as much cargo, Clippers are versatile ships that can be used as passenger transports, couriers, or even warships in a pinch, and they come with enough free space to be customized based on an individual captain's needs.

variants.name: Clipper (KGS Twin Gatling)
variants.description: The Clipper is the smaller sister ship to the popular Southbound Shipyards Argosy. Although not able to carry as much cargo, Clippers are versatile ships that can be used as passenger transports, couriers, or even warships in a pinch, and they come with enough free space to be customized based on an individual captain's needs.

variants.name: Clipper (KGS Staggered Twin Gatling)
variants.description: The Clipper is the smaller sister ship to the popular Southbound Shipyards Argosy. Although not able to carry as much cargo, Clippers are versatile ships that can be used as passenger transports, couriers, or even warships in a pinch, and they come with enough free space to be customized based on an individual captain's needs.

variants.name: Clipper (KGS Strike)
variants.description: The Clipper is the smaller sister ship to the popular Southbound Shipyards Argosy. Although not able to carry as much cargo, Clippers are versatile ships that can be used as passenger transports, couriers, or even warships in a pinch, and they come with enough free space to be customized based on an individual captain's needs.

variants.name: Behemoth (KGS Particle Turret)
variants.description: The Behemoth is a recent ship design from Betelgeuse Shipyards, designed as an alternative to the Syndicate Bulk Freighter. Although its cargo capacity is somewhat lower, it is also more maneuverable, and packs a heavier armament by default.

variants.name: Bactrian (KGS Particle Turret)
variants.description: The Lionheart Bactrian is the last of the great city-ships, a design hearkening back to the early days of space colonization when a long-distance vessel needed to be a self-contained world, able to survive for weeks at a time without encountering an inhabited planet. It is not only a freighter, but a carrier, and a capable warship either at short range or at a distance. 	Naturally, this versatility also makes it extremely expensive, and the Bactrian is normally only for sale to citizens who have been vetted by the local government of the Deep.

variants.name: Fury (KGS Storm)
variants.description: The Fury is Southbound Shipyards' most popular design of escort ship. They have greater firepower than any other interceptor-class vessel, meaning that any pirate flying solo will think twice before attacking a convoy that is accompanied by a Fury. However, Furies are also much less maneuverable than other small ships.

variants.name: Hawk (KGS Javelin Launcher)
variants.description: The Tarazed Hawk is an interceptor-class warship often used as an escort for freighters or in a planetary militia's patrol squadron. Hawks are fast enough to chase down most smaller ships, but are capable of mounting much more powerful weapons. They are also, of course, a favorite ship of pirate captains who have earned enough money to afford one.

variants.name: KGS Maker (Mark II)
variants.description: A Rainmaker without its missile launchers, the Maker is a sturdy but sluggish interceptor. Because of its decreased firepower, the Maker is available to regular civilians for purchase.

variants.name: KGS Maker (Mark II Old Weapons)
variants.description: A Rainmaker without its missile launchers, the Maker is a sturdy but sluggish interceptor. Because of its decreased firepower, the Maker is available to regular civilians for purchase.

variants.name: Headhunter (KGS Particle Carronade)
variants.description: The Headhunter is one of the newest ships designed by Lionheart: a versatile ship that can serve equally well for carrying cargo or for bounty hunting. As with many of Lionheart's designs, the hull is mostly composite fiber rather than metal, sacrificing armor strength for the sake of lighter weight and faster movement.

variants.name: Modified Argosy (KGS Javelin Launcher)
variants.description: The Argosy has long been a favorite ship for pirates, who have come up with many tricks for modifying them to be more suitable as warships.

variants.name: Modified Argosy (KGS Pure Gatling)
variants.description: The Argosy has long been a favorite ship for pirates, who have come up with many tricks for modifying them to be more suitable as warships.

variants.name: Raven (KGS Particle Carronade)
variants.description: The Lionheart Raven is their most popular warship, an agile and elegant vessel that can nevertheless deal out and absorb considerable damage. Ravens are a favorite ship for bounty hunters, because they are more than a match for pirate interceptors and can be used by a skilled pilot to take down much larger ships as well.

variants.name: KGS Dipper (Heavy)
variants.description: The Dipper is a light warship and freighter cobbled together from two Clipper hulls. Seven gun ports and a spacious cargo hold make it a desirable ship among merchants and pirates in the South.

variants.name: KGS Mistmaker (Mark II)
variants.description: The Mistmaker is lighter version of the Rainmaker missile boat, built for civilian use. It comes equipped with four missile pods and enough ammunition to destroy even ships many times its size, and is also slightly faster than the Rainmaker.

variants.name: Aerie (KGS Dual Particle Cannon)
variants.description: The Lionheart Aerie is a light carrier, designed to be just big enough for four fighter bays plus a decent armament of its own. Variations on this same ship design have been in use in the Deep for almost half a millennium, but this model comes with the very latest in generator and weapon technology.

variants.name: Bastion (KGS Brawler)
variants.description: The Southbound Shipyards Bastion is designed as a heavy defense platform to accompany freighter convoys, in addition to having enough cargo capacity to serve as a freighter in its own right. Because of its heavy armament, it is classified as a warship rather than a freighter.

variants.name: KGS Eyrie (KGS Dual Particle Cannon)
variants.description: The Lionheart Eyrie is a light carrier derived from the Aerie. The Aerie's four internal fighter bays have been rearranged into six drone bays, which are commonly found carrying Lionheart's Stiletto drones.

variants.name: Frigate (KGS Particle Howitzer)
variants.description: For centuries, the Frigate was the largest warship produced by the Republic Navy Yard, but now they seem quite small compared to a Cruiser or a Carrier. Armed with a variety of weapons, the Frigate is equally capable at fending off small fighters or bombarding larger ships. In its time it possessed relatively good maneuverability for a ship of its size, but now it sees continued use mostly due to the sheer number of them already built for service.

variants.name: Frigate (KGS Particle Howitzer Mark II)
variants.description: For centuries, the Frigate was the largest warship produced by the Republic Navy Yard, but now they seem quite small compared to a Cruiser or a Carrier. Armed with a variety of weapons, the Frigate is equally capable at fending off small fighters or bombarding larger ships. In its time it possessed relatively good maneuverability for a ship of its size, but now it sees continued use mostly due to the sheer number of them already built for service.

variants.name: KGS Eyas (Heavy)
variants.description: A shortened Kingfisher, the Eyas-class Destroyer is the fastest medium warship in the Southern Rim, although it is somewhat undergunned.

variants.name: KGS Stormmaker (Mark II)
variants.description: The Stormmaker is a heavy missile platform derived from the Rainmaker missile boat. It comes equipped with twelve missile launchers and enough ammunition to destroy ships many times its size. However, it is still slow, and its backup non-missile armament is still very weak.

variants.name: Cruiser (KGS Dual Particle Cannon)
variants.description: The Republic Cruiser is a relatively recent design, a much larger version of the Frigate designed to be able to take on an entire pirate fleet if necessary. In addition to a wide variety of weaponry, Cruisers have space to carry four attack drones: fast, pilotless fighters that can be controlled remotely.

variants.name: Cruiser (KGS Dual Particle Cannon Mark II)
variants.description: The Republic Cruiser is a relatively recent design, a much larger version of the Frigate designed to be able to take on an entire pirate fleet if necessary. In addition to a wide variety of weaponry, Cruisers have space to carry four attack drones: fast, pilotless fighters that can be controlled remotely.
variants.variant: Cruiser (KGS Dual Particle Cannon Mark II)

variants.name: Cruiser (KGS Particle Turret)
variants.description: The Republic Cruiser is a relatively recent design, a much larger version of the Frigate designed to be able to take on an entire pirate fleet if necessary. In addition to a wide variety of weaponry, Cruisers have space to carry four attack drones: fast, pilotless fighters that can be controlled remotely.

variants.name: Cruiser (KGS Particle Turret Mark II)
variants.description: The Republic Cruiser is a relatively recent design, a much larger version of the Frigate designed to be able to take on an entire pirate fleet if necessary. In addition to a wide variety of weaponry, Cruisers have space to carry four attack drones: fast, pilotless fighters that can be controlled remotely.

variants.name: Cruiser (KGS Raindrop)
variants.description: The Republic Cruiser is a relatively recent design, a much larger version of the Frigate designed to be able to take on an entire pirate fleet if necessary. In addition to a wide variety of weaponry, Cruisers have space to carry four attack drones: fast, pilotless fighters that can be controlled remotely.

variants.name: Cruiser (KGS Raindrop Mark II)
variants.description: The Republic Cruiser is a relatively recent design, a much larger version of the Frigate designed to be able to take on an entire pirate fleet if necessary. In addition to a wide variety of weaponry, Cruisers have space to carry four attack drones: fast, pilotless fighters that can be controlled remotely.

variants.name: Carrier (KGS Particle Turret)
variants.description: The Carrier is more than just a warship: it is practically a mobile space station, with docking bays for an entire squadron of drones and fighters and enough crew to fill a small village. Although designed primarily for engaging enemies from a distance, a Carrier can also do significant damage in close combat.

variants.name: Carrier (KGS Particle Turret Mark II)
variants.description: The Carrier is more than just a warship: it is practically a mobile space station, with docking bays for an entire squadron of drones and fighters and enough crew to fill a small village. Although designed primarily for engaging enemies from a distance, a Carrier can also do significant damage in close combat.

variants.name: Leviathan (KGS Particle Burster)
variants.description: The Betelgeuse Shipyards Leviathan has been in service since long before the Republic existed. Although considered today to be an outdated and clunky model, it is still a force to be reckoned with.

variants.name: KGS Kingfisher (II)
variants.description: A Falcon without its side pods, the Kingfisher-class Battlecruiser is faster and even sleeker, at the cost of weapon space and hardpoints. It has been adapted to use strike tactics, flying fast at the target and unloading a flurry of plasma fire and Javelins before retreating for another dive.

variants.name: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)
variants.description: A Falcon without its side pods, the Kingfisher-class Battlecruiser is faster and even sleeker, at the cost of weapon space and hardpoints. It has been adapted to use strike tactics, flying fast at the target and unloading a flurry of plasma fire and Javelins before retreating for another dive.
variants.variant: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

variants.name: KGS Kookaburra (Quad Blaster)
variants.description: Tarazed Shipyards created this monstrosity as a competitor to the Syndicate Protector. A Kingfisher has been stretched, and 10 extra turrets have been added. What it lacks in affordability it makes up for in mobility - upgraded engines allow it to go alarmingly fast for a 12 turret ship.

variants.name: Dropship (KGS Mini Blaster)
variants.description: Dropships were designed with the Navy's Auxiliary in mind. Landed Auxiliaries often act as forward operating bases, so troops needing transport to another area often rely on their Dropships. This cramped and uncomfortable vessel is able to move a squad of Republic soldiers at great speed for its size. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Finch (KGS Javelin Storm Pod)
variants.description: When the Free Worlds started requesting a Carrier solution from its local shipyards, Tarazed Shipyards quickly saw that a few modifications could make their entry level interceptor into a top tier fighter. Over 85% of the parts that make up the Finch are shared with the Sparrow assembly line, so it's no surprise that even trimmed down to (barely) fit into a fighter bay, the blindingly fast Finch bears strong resemblance to its hyperspace-faring cousin. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Finch (KGS Javelin Strike Pod)
variants.description: When the Free Worlds started requesting a Carrier solution from its local shipyards, Tarazed Shipyards quickly saw that a few modifications could make their entry level interceptor into a top tier fighter. Over 85% of the parts that make up the Finch are shared with the Sparrow assembly line, so it's no surprise that even trimmed down to (barely) fit into a fighter bay, the blindingly fast Finch bears strong resemblance to its hyperspace-faring cousin. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Finch (KGS Gatling Snubnose)
variants.description: When the Free Worlds started requesting a Carrier solution from its local shipyards, Tarazed Shipyards quickly saw that a few modifications could make their entry level interceptor into a top tier fighter. Over 85% of the parts that make up the Finch are shared with the Sparrow assembly line, so it's no surprise that even trimmed down to (barely) fit into a fighter bay, the blindingly fast Finch bears strong resemblance to its hyperspace-faring cousin. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: Lance (KGS Particle Carronade)
variants.description: The Lance is the primary fighter used by the Republic Navy. As with all fighters, it is weak by itself but very effective as part of a larger squadron. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: KGS Raindrop (Mark II)
variants.description: The Raindrop is a light missile drone built in the Republic Navy Yard, equipped with two missile launchers. Because of its extremely frail hull and lack of shielding, Raindrops serve best as long-range attack drones, and once their missile supply is spent, they are useless in combat.

variants.name: Lor'kas Ik 577 (KGS Double Digger)
variants.description: The largest of Korath production vessels are derived from a common and ancient chassis. The simplest are adapted to haul materials between refinery and factory stations. Fittingly, they're named after a productive colony of the Efreti, to honor their focus on peaceful industry.

variants.name: Rano'erek (KGS Double Digger)
variants.description: After the Korath were exiled from their homelands, efficient resource collection became a priority. World-ships were too precious to risk on dangerous forays, and raiders lacked the cargo capacity. The Rano'erek-class Asteroid Dredging Ship, named for a respected civilian politician and economist, was born as an answer to this problem. 	Featuring an abundance of cargo and passenger space in addition to their formidable defensive armament, these bulky ships can also offer potent support for frontline Palavrets.

variants.name: KGS Eyas (Kai)
variants.description: A shortened Kingfisher, the Eyas-class Destroyer is the fastest medium warship in the Southern Rim, although it is somewhat undergunned.

variants.name: KGS Silver Gull (Sniper)
variants.description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Gull (Support)
variants.description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Gull (Cargo)
variants.description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Gull (Bunks)
variants.description: While the Starling was the first successful ship to use technology derived from alien ruins, the Gull is the most recent. The current increase in Korath activities has caused foraging for resources to become a risky endeavor, forcing the Remnant to develop a specialized freighter for hauling cargo from remote sites without revealing their presence to hostile aliens. 	The Gull has quickly proven its worth, allowing the Starling fleets to return to riskier activities while the Gull takes over the hazardous convoy routes. It is now the primary element of the Remnant's extra-territorial resource operations. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Gull's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Pelican (KGS Twinhibitor)
variants.description: The Pelican is closely based on the original semi-organic materials the Remnant found in the Ember Waste. While they are not technically alive, their internal mechanisms and method of construction bear such a close resemblance to living organisms that they are often considered as such. One key feature of this living resemblance is the innate sensory capabilities they possess, akin to sensor suites less advanced ships require for detailed scanning. These ships serve as the backbone of Remnant bulk salvage efforts, as well as their fast-logistics distribution network.

variants.name: KGS Silver Pelican (Heavy Lift)
variants.description: The Pelican is closely based on the original semi-organic materials the Remnant found in the Ember Waste. While they are not technically alive, their internal mechanisms and method of construction bear such a close resemblance to living organisms that they are often considered as such. One key feature of this living resemblance is the innate sensory capabilities they possess, akin to sensor suites less advanced ships require for detailed scanning. These ships serve as the backbone of Remnant bulk salvage efforts, as well as their fast-logistics distribution network.

variants.name: KGS Silver Pelican (KGS Twinhibitor)
variants.description: The Pelican is closely based on the original semi-organic materials the Remnant found in the Ember Waste. While they are not technically alive, their internal mechanisms and method of construction bear such a close resemblance to living organisms that they are often considered as such. One key feature of this living resemblance is the innate sensory capabilities they possess, akin to sensor suites less advanced ships require for detailed scanning. These ships serve as the backbone of Remnant bulk salvage efforts, as well as their fast-logistics distribution network.

variants.name: Merganser (KGS Hybrid)
variants.description: The Merganser is the first production ship the Remnant deem to be truly alive, and are considered to be equivalent to dolphins in reasoning tests. These interceptors, deemed for short range operations by Remnant standards, are designed to patrol the upper atmosphere of gas giants and use their decisive speed advantage to conduct hit-and-run attacks on threats in nearby systems. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Merganser's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Merganser (KGS Bomber)
variants.description: The Merganser is the first production ship the Remnant deem to be truly alive, and are considered to be equivalent to dolphins in reasoning tests. These interceptors, deemed for short range operations by Remnant standards, are designed to patrol the upper atmosphere of gas giants and use their decisive speed advantage to conduct hit-and-run attacks on threats in nearby systems. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Merganser's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.description: The Merganser is the first production ship the Remnant deem to be truly alive, and are considered to be equivalent to dolphins in reasoning tests. The cloaking technology is built into the Merganser's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Merganser (KGS Bomber)
variants.description: The Merganser is the first production ship the Remnant deem to be truly alive, and are considered to be equivalent to dolphins in reasoning tests. These interceptors, deemed for short range operations by Remnant standards, are designed to patrol the upper atmosphere of gas giants and use their decisive speed advantage to conduct hit-and-run attacks on threats in nearby systems. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Merganser's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Starling (Heavy)
variants.description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Starling (Sniper)
variants.description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Starling (Hunter)
variants.description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: KGS Silver Starling (Thrasher)
variants.description: One of the strangest and most useful things that the Remnant have discovered in the abandoned worlds of the Ember Waste is alien cloaking technology. They have designed their scout ships to cloak when needed in order to explore alien territory without being seen... or to escape in a hurry after stealing something particularly valuable from those aliens. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Starling's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Ibis (KGS Heavy)
variants.description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

variants.name: Ibis (KGS Twinhibitor)
variants.description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

variants.name: KGS Silver Ibis (KGS Heavy)
variants.description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

variants.name: KGS Silver Ibis (KGS Twinhibitor)
variants.description: The Ibis is a multipurpose carrier that serves as a mobile mining platform, an escort ship for Pelican fleets, and a combat platform using Terns for screening. It is capable of fielding a single wing of support craft, consisting of two Petrels and four Terns. 	The Ibis was designed shortly after the Albatross as a means of extending the range of resource collection efforts after the Remnant realized that an Alpha invasion might not be as imminent as originally believed.

variants.name: KGS Silver Peregrine (II)
variants.description: The Peregrine is the first capital warship designed by Taely using fresh insights garnered from recent experimentation as well as renewed studies of the void sprites. Encompassing over a century of development since the last generation of warships was built, it is the forerunner of a new breed of Remnant ships. 	The Peregrine was designed along the lines of old-earth's fast-attack cruisers. Drawing on inspiration from its namesake, famed for being the fastest self-propelled lifeform on Earth, it is designed to strike hard and fast. It closes quickly with enemies to unleash devastating barrages in the opening seconds of combat. 	The cloaking technology is built into the Peregrine's hull; it cannot be removed or transferred to another ship.

variants.name: Albatross (KGS Twinhibitor)
variants.description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

variants.name: Albatross (KGS Sniper)
variants.description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

variants.name: KGS Silver Albatross (KGS Sniper)
variants.description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

variants.description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

variants.name: KGS Silver Albatross (KGS Trithrasher)
variants.description: Once they left human space, it became apparent to the Remnant that they would need to build their own shipyards in order to defend themselves if they were discovered by the Alphas or any other unfriendly faction. Using new composite materials that they discovered, they built ships very different from anything seen in human space.

variants.name: KGS Silver Smew (Miner)
variants.description: The Remnant have long favored turns of action that avoid putting crew directly at risk; though their Terns are a rather effective result of this ideology, the Remnant still had to face the fact that, eventually, one of their manned ships was going to end up too crippled to escape to safety. To that end, the Smew was developed; though quite lightly armed, Smews are capable of carrying a considerable quantity of cargo and passengers for a ship of their size, and are durable enough to absorb some fire from enemies while doing so. 	Though originally designed as lifeboats, the surprisingly mobile freighter-fighter hybrid has seen a remarkable amount of usage in mining fleets, as a way to supplement the otherwise poor cargo capacity of the Ibis. Because their purpose can vary from fleet to fleet, Smews are traditionally sold with a fair amount of free space: enough for a small weapon, upgraded engines, or additional support systems. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.name: KGS Silver Smew (Runabout)
variants.description: The Remnant have long favored turns of action that avoid putting crew directly at risk; though their Terns are a rather effective result of this ideology, the Remnant still had to face the fact that, eventually, one of their manned ships was going to end up too crippled to escape to safety. To that end, the Smew was developed; though quite lightly armed, Smews are capable of carrying a considerable quantity of cargo and passengers for a ship of their size, and are durable enough to absorb some fire from enemies while doing so. 	Though originally designed as lifeboats, the surprisingly mobile freighter-fighter hybrid has seen a remarkable amount of usage in mining fleets, as a way to supplement the otherwise poor cargo capacity of the Ibis. Because their purpose can vary from fleet to fleet, Smews are traditionally sold with a fair amount of free space: enough for a small weapon, upgraded engines, or additional support systems. 	Fighters do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a fighter unless you have a ship in your fleet with a fighter bay.

variants.description: The Remnant have long favored turns of action that avoid putting crew directly at risk; though their Terns are a rather effective result of this ideology, the Remnant still had to face the fact that, eventually, one of their manned ships was going to end up too crippled to escape to safety. Because their purpose can vary from fleet to fleet, Smews are traditionally sold with a fair amount of free space: enough for a small weapon, upgraded engines, or additional support systems. variants.name: KGS Rapid Stream 0
variants.description: Developed after the Unfettered invasion, the Rapid Stream is a cut down Deep River, equipped with three turrets for extra defense from Unfettered Trackers.

variants.name: KGS Rapid Stream 1
variants.description: Developed after the Unfettered invasion, the Rapid Stream is a cut down Deep River, equipped with three turrets for extra defense from Unfettered Trackers.

variants.name: KGS Rapid Stream 2
variants.description: Developed after the Unfettered invasion, the Rapid Stream is a cut down Deep River, equipped with three turrets for extra defense from Unfettered Trackers.

variants.name: KGS Rapid Stream 3
variants.description: Developed after the Unfettered invasion, the Rapid Stream is a cut down Deep River, equipped with three turrets for extra defense from Unfettered Trackers.

variants.name: KGS Rapid Stream 4
variants.description: Developed after the Unfettered invasion, the Rapid Stream is a cut down Deep River, equipped with three turrets for extra defense from Unfettered Trackers.

outfits.name: KGS 1x1 VLS
outfits.description: Although the 9, 18, and 36 cell variants of the Avgi's Vertical Launch Array are by far the most common, their modular nature allows them to be easily made in all manner of odd sizes. This is the cell that all Avgi vertical launch systems are built from. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cell's contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This cell's negligible footprint makes it suitable for stuffing into nooks and crannies left over from other weapons. Sadly, the system still requires a turret mount. 	These tiny vertical launch cells enable versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 1x2 VLS
outfits.description: This extremely tiny vertical launch system contains two independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's extremely compact footprint makes it even more suitable for drones and fighters than the four cell version, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 2x2 VLS
outfits.description: This very tiny vertical launch system contains four independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's very compact footprint makes it even more suitable for drones and fighters than the six cell version, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 2x3 VLS
outfits.description: This tiny vertical launch system contains six independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's compact footprint makes it even more suitable for drones and fighters than the nine cell version, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 3x4 VLS
outfits.description: This tiny vertical launch system contains twelve independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's compact footprint makes it suitable for drones and fighters, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 3x5 VLS
outfits.description: This tiny vertical launch system contains fifteen independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's compact footprint makes it suitable for drones and fighters, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 4x6 VLS
outfits.description: This small vertical launch system contains 24 independent launch cells in a rectangular array. Designed for flexibility, the system can employ a variety of small or medium-sized missiles. Owing to its large surface footprint, a turret mount is required for the launcher to deploy missiles safely. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 5x6 VLS
outfits.description: This small vertical launch system contains 30 independent launch cells in a rectangular array. Designed for flexibility, the system can employ a variety of small or medium-sized missiles. Owing to its large surface footprint, a turret mount is required for the launcher to deploy missiles safely. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 6x8 VLS
outfits.description: This medium vertical launch system contains 48 independent launch cells arrayed in parallel. A wide variety of small to mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the launch cells, which can be easily interchanged. Designed for swift vertical cold-launch, the system needs an open space comparable to a turret mount to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 6x10 VLS
outfits.description: This medium vertical launch system contains 60 independent launch cells arrayed in parallel. A wide variety of small to mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the launch cells, which can be easily interchanged. Designed for swift vertical cold-launch, the system needs an open space comparable to a turret mount to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 6x12 VLS
outfits.description: This large vertical launch system contains 72 independent launch cells in a rectangular formation. A variety of small and mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the interchangeable launch cells. Designed for quick vertical launch, this system needs a turret mount for the required open space to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 9x12 VLS
outfits.description: This large vertical launch system contains 108 independent launch cells in a rectangular formation. A variety of small and mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the interchangeable launch cells. Designed for quick vertical launch, this system needs a turret mount for the required open space to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 12x12 VLS
outfits.description: This huge vertical launch system contains 144 independent launch cells in a rectangular formation. The interchangeable launch cells are compatible with a variety of small and mid-sized Avgi missiles. Designed for speedy vertical launch, this system requires a large turret mount to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Twin Mandible Cannon
outfits.description: These two Mandible Cannons have been bolted together, creating a devastating long range weapon.

outfits.name: KGS Hyperpulse Cannon
outfits.description: The Hyperpulse Cannon is a more recent development of the Hai, a Pulse Cannon adapted to fire projectiles with much higher velocity, accuracy, and damage.

outfits.name: KGS Bipulse Ripper
outfits.description: The Unfettered have joined two Pulse Cannons, resulting in an effective and efficient weapon.

outfits.name: KGS Twin Railgun
outfits.description: The twin Skipper Railgun is favored by pilots who appreciate the ion and scrambling damage of the regular Skipper Railgun, but find it too small for use on ships larger than a drone. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Triple Railgun
outfits.description: This weapon is, quite simply, the internals of three Skipper Railguns in a larger frame. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Quad Railgun
outfits.description: Many captains have seen the power of the Skipper Railgun, and wanted a version fit for use on larger ships. This is the result - four Skipper Railguns in a larger housing. The recoil is intense. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 2x3 Railgun
outfits.description: The recoil from this weapon probably renders reverse thrusters unnecessary. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 3x3 Railgun
outfits.description: Only the most deranged of captains use these. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 3x4 Railgun
outfits.description: The few captains who use these swear that flying backwards with them make engines unnecessary. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 4x4 Railgun
outfits.description: The creator was drunk on acorn liquor when this was designed... 	Due to the huge fire rate, it was impossible to synchronize the firing of this weapon. As a result, it fires twice every shot. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 4x5 Railgun
outfits.description: Why not? 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Mini Blaster
outfits.description: The Energy Blaster has long been popular for its small size, allowing it to be stuffed almost anywhere. The Mini Blaster is an attempt to capitalize on this, featuring an even smaller footprint, but even more disappointing performance. 	As with the Energy Blaster, it is decently effective in large numbers.

outfits.name: KGS Particle Carronade
outfits.description: The Particle Carronade is a miniaturized Particle Cannon that fires smaller particle bolts, tuned for higher damage and a more rapid fire rate. Range and accuracy are reduced, but the small bolts allow for excellent velocity, and is thus a popular anti-fighter weapon.

outfits.name: KGS Particle Howitzer
outfits.description: The Particle Howitzer is a scaled-up Particle Cannon, firing larger particle bursts with higher velocity. It has better range and damage than the Particle Cannon, but fires more slowly, and is also much larger.

outfits.name: KGS Particle Burster
outfits.description: The pinnacle of pirate technology, the Particle Burster is a hodgepodge of two Particle Carronades and some Proton Gun components, among other various odds and ends. As its name suggests, it fires particle bolts in bursts, and is commonly found equipped on the flagships of various pirate warlords, for facing off with other gangs or the authorities. The bolts fired by this weapon are of lower accuracy and velocity than the Particle Carronade's, but are modified for more damage.

outfits.name: KGS Dual Particle Cannon
outfits.description: The Dual Particle Cannon is incredibly space-efficient for its firepower. However, it is less accurate, consumes more energy and creates quite a lot more heat than two Particle Cannons.

Ships: Cruiser (KGS Dual Particle Cannon Mark II)

outfits.name: KGS Plasma Assault Repeater
outfits.description: The new main weapon of the Kingfisher fast heavy warship, the Plasma Assault Repeater is a Plasma Repeater adapted to fire in bursts. The modifications resulted in slightly increased overall damage, at the cost of increased energy use and heat generation.

outfits.name: KGS Particle Turret
outfits.description: The Lovelace Particle Turret is a new product designed for the heavy anti-capital role, mounting two powerful Particle Cannons on a rotating base. It has also become popular as an anti-fighter weapon for its excellent range and projectile velocity.

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod
outfits.description: A Javelin Mini Pod cut down to just a single tube, the Javelin Micro Pod is so small it is almost useless. However, it can fit nearly anywhere. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Launcher
outfits.description: This is simply two Javelin Pods welded together to fit in a single gun port. It is favored by captains who have sufficient space, but insufficient hardpoints to mount Javelin Pods. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Storm Pod
outfits.description: The Javelin Mini Pod has long been a favorite of fighter pilots for its high damage and fire rate. The Javelin Storm Pod is a pair of Javelin Mini Pods welded together, but with a twist: it fires its eight tubes very, very rapidly, dealing great burst damage. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Storm Launcher
outfits.description: The Javelin Storm Launcher, developed by Kraz Cybernetics for use on Tarazed's Kingfisher battlecruiser, fires all its 24 tubes very quickly, perfect for the Kingfisher's strike tactics. 	The weapon's extremely concentrated burst fire makes it almost impossible for anti-missile systems to counter all the missiles, ensuring that at least some of them hit. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Strike Pod
outfits.description: The Javelin Strike Pod is a Javelin Mini Pod adapted to fire modified Strike Javelins - regular Javelins with a much larger propellant section. 	Unfortunately, the systems that enable the three-shot burst fire on the full-size launcher couldn't fit on this pod. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Strike Launcher
outfits.description: The Javelin Strike Launcher is a Javelin Pod adapted to fire modified Strike Javelins - regular Javelins with a much larger propellant section. 	To maximize the chance of a hit at long range, this weapon has been adapted to fire in bursts of three. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer
outfits.description: The Javelin Pod's greatest strength is in its numbers, and thus this weapon exemplifies that further. Tiny Javelin Micro Pods, stripped down to the bare minimum loading magazine with small maneuvering thrusters for aiming correction, are launched in considerable number, providing a sustained fire rate equal to that of 6 Javelin Pods at the cost of massively increased tonnage overhead. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

outfits.name: KGS Twin Gatling
outfits.description: These two Gatling Guns have been crudely joined to fit into a single gun port, offering twice as much damage but twice the power consumption and heat, along with a little bit of extra mass. However, it still needs to cool down for twice as long as it fires, and can only sustain a maximum of three seconds of continuous fire. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Staggered Twin Gatling
outfits.description: These two Gatling Guns have been crudely joined to fit into a single gun port, offering twice as much damage but twice the heat and power consumption, along with a little bit of extra mass. However, an intricate mechanism staggers the fire of the two guns. It needs to cool down for half as long as it fires, and can only sustain a maximum of six seconds of continuous fire. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Triple Gatling
outfits.description: These three Gatling Guns have been joined rather more nicely than the pirate-made Twin Gatling. An ingenious device staggers the fire of all three guns, allowing for sustained continuous fire. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Quad Gatling
outfits.description: This scary weapon has been cobbled together from 4 Gatling Guns. Barmy Edward took inspiration from the Staggered Gatling Gun made and used by pirates, and this is the result - a weapon with huge damage potential, large enough to be used by capital ships. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS 2x3 Gatling
outfits.description: This absurd weapon was created by Barmy Edward. When asked why he designed it, he simply grins. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Quad Gatling Turret
outfits.description: A pirate warlord has had two Staggered Twin Gatling Guns attached to a turret mount. Needless to say, the result is a terror, with a much faster cooldown time in between bursts than the original Gatling Turret. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Gatling Snubnose
outfits.description: A creative pirate has chopped off most of the barrel of a Gatling Gun, resulting in a much lighter and faster firing weapon. However, the downsides are much diminished range, accuracy, and muzzle velocity, combined with a lower thermal limit and a hefty cooldown time. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS High Velocity Gatling Gun
outfits.description: A creative pirate has gone in the opposite direction of the Gatling Snubnose, and has instead lengthened the barrel of the Gatling Gun, resulting in higher muzzle velocity and accuracy. The longer barrel also results in a higher thermal limit and more effective cooling, allowing it to fire continuously, though at a lower rate because of the increased barrel weight. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

outfits.name: KGS Twin Flamethrower
outfits.description: The already-fearsome Flamethrower has been made doubly scary with this twinned version. Two Flamethrowers have been joined in a fashion that allows for slightly better fuel efficiency at the cost of heat.

outfits.name: KGS Strike Javelin
outfits.description: The Strike Javelin missile is ammunition for the Javelin Strike Launcher; without a launcher to store them in you cannot purchase or use Strike Javelins.

outfits.name: KGS Strike Javelin Storage Crate
outfits.description: The Strike Javelin Storage Crate is used to store extra ammunition for Javelin Strike Launchers.

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod Package
outfits.description: This package is a stripped-down Javelin Micro Pod, with a minimized loading magazine holding 6 Javelins, and a few small chemical thrusters for aiming. The Javelin Micro Pod Package is ammunition for the Javelin Micro Pod Deployer; without a deployer to store them in you cannot purchase or use Javelin Micro Pod Packages.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

outfits.name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod Storage Rack
outfits.description: The Javelin Micro Pod Storage Rack is used to store extra ammunition for Javelin Micro Pod Deployers.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

outfits.name: KGS Binary Repeater
outfits.description: Two Thermal Repeaters have been wired together, resulting in rather insane heat output.

outfits.name: KGS Double Digger Mining Beam
outfits.description: These two Digger Mining Beams have been stacked. Some clever engineering allows it to be slightly more effective than two separate beams, at the cost of a requiring little more energy and making a little more heat.

outfits.name: KGS Double Digger Mining Turret
outfits.description: The complement to the Double Digger Mining Beam, this handy turret, originally meant for asteroid mining, is actually quite effective against ships due to its shield disruption capabilities.

outfits.name: KGS Twinhibitor
outfits.description: Simply two Inhibitor Cannons in a single frame, this weapon is favored by Remnant captains trying to fit the maximum number of the long-range, slowing weapons as possible on their ship.

outfits.name: KGS Bithrasher
outfits.description: The Bithrasher was created when several Remnant captains expressed dissatisfaction because filling their ships with Thrasher Cannons left a lot of weapon space free.

outfits.name: KGS Trithrasher
outfits.description: The Trithrasher is for captains who want even more than the Twin Thrasher. It does incredible damage up close, especially against hull.
